paper_title: Diatom Paleolimnology Data Collection and Processing

methods.description: Process for collecting diatom paleolimnology datasets from various sources, including EPA and INSTAAR, to study lake conditions across multiple states.

steps.description: Five diatom paleolimnology datasets with sample-specific counts and lake characteristics from 8 states were downloaded from the online repositories of the United States Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) and from the University of Colorado Institute of Arctic and Alpine Research (INSTAAR) websites, or obtained from the State Agencies, EPA staff, and by querying the internal databases of the Academy of Natural Sciences of Drexel University (ANS).
steps.searchable_summary: download diatom paleolimnology datasets from EPA and INSTAAR repositories

methods.description: Procedure for collecting sediment samples using the 'top-bottom' sampling scheme to analyze present-day and pre-industrial lake conditions.

steps.description: Most samples were collected as part of the 'top-bottom' sediment sampling scheme that involves collecting a relatively short (~20-50 cm) sediment core and retaining only the 'top' and 'bottom' intervals.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment core samples using 'top-bottom' scheme

methods.description: Process for creating unique sample identifiers for datasets with non-unique identifiers.

steps.description: To create a unique sample identifier for EMAP samples, we concatenated contents of the following fields: 'LAKE_ID', 'YEAR' (year of sampling), 'VISIT_NO' (visit number) and 'COREPOS' ('top' or 'bottom' position of the sample in the core).
steps.searchable_summary: concatenate fields to create unique sample identifiers

methods.description: Procedure for translating originally reported diatom names to their current nomenclatural synonyms for consistency across datasets.

steps.description: Originally reported names were first translated to their current nomenclatural synonyms.
steps.searchable_summary: translate diatom names to current nomenclatural synonyms
